issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The register() function for dropdown menus adds aria-controls to the trigger but never sets aria-expanded when the panel is opened or closed, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and in _closeInstance() add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); so the trigger reflects the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels lack aria‑hidden updates
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is hidden the code sets the hidden attribute but never adds aria-hidden="true". Likewise, when shown it removes hidden without removing aria-hidden, so the element remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance() add setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in _openInstance() add removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden'); to keep the accessibility tree in sync.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels missing aria‑hidden updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ panels are hidden via the hidden attribute but the code never adds aria-hidden="true". When shown the hidden attribute is removed but aria-hidden remains, leaving hidden content exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In close(item) add setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open(item) add removeAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden'); to correctly manage visibility in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button _navToggle toggles the mobile drawer but never updates aria‑pressed to indicate its pressed state, violating the ARIA button pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true'); and in mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false'); so the button’s pressed state is announced.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The _navToggle element controls the drawer _navDrawer but no aria-controls attribute is set, breaking the relationship between trigger and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init() after locating _trigger and _drawer, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')); to expose the control relationship.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (data‑period) receive a visual .active class but never set aria‑pressed, so screen‑reader users cannot tell which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncToggleState() after toggling the CSS.active class, also call setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); for each button.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing toggle switch missing aria‑pressed and aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing toggle (data‑billing-toggle) updates visual state but does not set aria‑pressed or aria‑controls to indicate its state or the panel it controls.
issuesFound.fix: In _onToggleClick() add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(_pricingState.period === 'year')); and setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_toggleTrack, 'billing-toggle')); so the switch is properly described.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates (filter results) not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() updates the product count badge and empty‑state message but never writes to a live region, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, call liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`); or create a hidden <div role="status"> to receive the announcement.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened or closed the code emits custom events but does not write to an aria‑live region, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance() add liveRegion.announce(`Menu ${qs('[aria-label]', trigger).textContent.trim()} opened`); and in _closeInstance() announce the closed state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item triggers custom events but does not announce the change, so users of assistive technology are not informed that an answer has been revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In open(item) add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`); and in close(item) announce `${item.question} – answer collapsed`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter toggle buttons (view mode) missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view‑mode buttons (data‑view‑mode) toggle between grid and list layouts but never set aria‑pressed, so their toggle state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In _onViewToggle() after applying the view mode, call setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-view-mode')===_productFilterState.viewMode)); for each view‑mode button.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers do not update **aria‑expanded**
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed the script only adds/removes CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute on the panel. The trigger button never receives an `aria‑expanded` update, so assistive technologies cannot tell whether the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation toggle button never receives **aria‑expanded**
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) controls the off‑canvas drawer but the script never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` / `"false"` on it. Screen readers therefore cannot perceive the drawer’s state.

issuesFound.title: Search input does not toggle **aria‑expanded** when the suggestion list opens/closes
issuesFound.explanation: The `<input>` that controls the autocomplete list never receives `aria‑expanded` updates, leaving the relationship between the input and the list ambiguous for AT.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks **aria‑pressed** / **aria‑expanded** state
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off billing‑period switch (`_toggleEl`) is styled as a toggle but never receives an ARIA state attribute, so its current mode is invisible to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation never sets **aria‑invalid** on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the script adds an error message and CSS classes but never adds `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). AT users therefore cannot detect the error programmatically.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown hide routine does **not** set **aria‑hidden** on the panel
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden with `hidden` and CSS classes, but `aria-hidden` is never added, so the hidden menu may still be exposed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav open/close does **not** set **aria‑hidden** on the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer (`_drawer`) is shown/hidden via CSS classes only; no `aria-hidden` attribute is toggled, so screen readers may still read its content when it is visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search result‑count updates are **not announced** via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The status `<div>` receives text but is never given `role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` (the element is created with `aria-live="polite"` earlier, but the function replaces its `textContent` without ensuring the live region is updated after the DOM change, and the element is hidden when there are no results, causing AT to miss the announcement.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does **not** announce state changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The script toggles `aria-expanded` on the trigger and adds CSS classes, but no live region is used to announce “Answer expanded” / “collapsed”. Users of screen readers rely on live announcements for dynamic disclosure widgets.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change does **not** announce the new pricing via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly/annual pricing the UI updates the numbers, but there is no ARIA live announcement, so AT users may not notice the price change.

fpTitles: Mobile‑nav open/close does **not** set **aria‑hidden** on the drawer element

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with `hidden` but not marked aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed the code sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` without also setting `aria-hidden="true"`. The element is removed from visual flow but remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
After adding `hidden`, also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove the attribute (`removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden')`).  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button lacks aria‑pressed state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) toggles the navigation drawer via `mobileNav.toggle()`, but the script never updates `aria‑pressed` to reflect the open/closed state, leaving screen‑reader users without feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open()` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; in `close()` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not set aria‑pressed or aria‑expanded on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The period selector buttons (`[data-period]`) are used as a radio‑style toggle, yet the code only adds a CSS `.active` class. No `aria‑pressed` (or `aria‑expanded`) is updated, so the current period is not announced to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
When a button is activated, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true')` and set the same attribute to `false` on all other buttons.  

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` uses `srUtils.markInvalid()` which only adds `aria-describedby`. It never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the offending `<input>`, `<select>` or `<textarea>`. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `srUtils.markInvalid()` add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true')`; in `srUtils.markValid()` remove that attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: The same `inlineValidator` clears only the error message but leaves `aria-invalid="true"` on fields that have been corrected, causing persistent error indication for AT users.
issuesFound.fix:
In `srUtils.markValid()` also call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are not announced via a live region on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` expands the panel with `animateHeight` and updates ARIA attributes, but no `aria-live` announcement is made. Users of screen readers receive no audible cue that a new answer has appeared.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened?.replace('{title}', item.question) || 'Answer expanded')`; similarly announce on close.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer visibility changes do not set aria‑hidden on the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is opened/closed the script adds/removes CSS classes and toggles `hidden`, but never updates `aria-hidden`. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree when hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open()` call `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden')`; in `close()` call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger elements lack initial aria‑expanded state on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown registration adds `aria-controls` but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button, leaving its collapsed state ambiguous to AT until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register()` after setting `aria-controls`, also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Several user‑initiated UI changes are not announced via a dedicated live region (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: After a search query the result count is updated in `_updateStatus`, but the status element is only `aria-live="polite"` and the text is overwritten without a specific announcement of the new count. Because the same region is reused for many unrelated messages, the change is not reliably announced.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the count, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchResultsFound?.replace('{n}', count) || `${count} results found`)`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new billing period to AT
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates the UI and price values but never announces the change (e.g., “Annual billing selected”). Users relying on screen readers are unaware of the switch.
issuesFound.fix:
After the price updates, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual?.replace('{period}', period) || `Billing period set to ${period}`)`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: Keyboard interaction (Enter/Space/Arrow) opens or closes a dropdown, but no `aria-live` message is emitted, so AT users receive no feedback that the menu expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
After calling `openInstance(inst)` or `closeInstance(inst)`, invoke `liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav open/close actions are not announced via a live region (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens or closes, the script emits custom events (`mobilenav:open` / `mobilenav:close`) but does not provide an `aria-live` announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the navigation state change.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce(open ? STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened' : STRINGS.navClose || 'Navigation menu closed')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel hide/show does not set aria‑hidden when hidden (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The `close(item)` function animates the panel and sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but never adds `aria-hidden="true"`. The hidden answer remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
After hiding, call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove that attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels are hidden with `hidden` but not removed from the accessibility tree on initial page load (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: Panels start with `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but lack `aria-hidden="true"`; AT may still expose them.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register()` after setting `hidden`, also call `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list is not announced via a dedicated live region when opened (JS‑G)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the suggestion panel only makes the list visible; no live‑region message is sent, so screen‑reader users are not told that suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix:
After `_open()`, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.searchSuggestionsOpen || 'Search suggestions available')`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer does not set aria‑hidden when closed (JS‑F)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes and `hidden` attribute, but `aria-hidden` is never set, leaving the element in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()`, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()`, remove that attribute.  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu trigger buttons lack `role="button"` when not a `<button>` (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: If a non‑button element (e.g., `<a>` or `<div>`) is used as a dropdown trigger, the script does not ensure it has `role="button"` and appropriate `tabindex`, which can make it inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register()`, after adding ARIA attributes, add `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('role')) setAttr(trigger, 'role', 'button'); if (!trigger.hasAttribute('tabindex')) setAttr(trigger, 'tabindex', '0');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle switch lacks `role="switch"` and `aria-checked` (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle (`_toggleEl`) is treated as a button but does not have `role="switch"` or `aria-checked`, which are required for AT to convey its binary state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `init()`, after locating `_toggleEl`, set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'role', 'switch')` and update `aria-checked` in `_syncToggleState`.  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack `role="region"` when the element is not a `<details>` (JS‑A)
issuesFound.explanation: The code adds `role="region"` only if the panel is not a `<details>` element, but does not verify that the element is focusable. Some panels may be `<div>` without `tabindex`, making them unreachable.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `role="region"`, also add `if (!panel.hasAttribute('tabindex')) setAttr(panel, 'tabindex', '-1');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: These functions control dropdown panel visibility by removing or adding the hidden attribute, but they do not set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button instances. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1854: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _openInstance. Line 1867: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion combobox is toggled open and closed, but the aria-expanded attribute on the input element is never updated to reflect the listbox visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2338: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _open. Line 2348: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _close.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as invalid, the function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field element. Screen readers cannot identify the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1603: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` before the closing brace of the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1614: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` at the end of the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the module initialises, leaving screen readers unaware that the menu is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1902: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after the event listener attachments in the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering new dropdown triggers, the code sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1949: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after setting the aria-controls attribute.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads with the suggestion list hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2405: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after setting up event listeners in the init function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count updates and the DOM changes, but no aria-live announcement is made to inform screen reader users how many products are now displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2648: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the prices update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2846: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes the visible content but does not announce the state change or the question text to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3041: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` in the open function. Line 3065: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element, preventing screen readers from identifying which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1902: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));` after selecting the drawer element.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle functions open() and close() add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the expanded or collapsed state. Screen readers will not announce the state change to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1584: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the open function after setting `_open = true;`. Line 1618: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the close function after setting `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion trigger missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open and close functions animate the panel and update internal state, but fail to toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the FAQ item.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3160: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the open function. Line 3186: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The site search combobox functions that open and close the suggestion panel do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input element. This violates the combobox pattern requirements and prevents assistive technologies from announcing the popup state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2678: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _open. Line 2690: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _close.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens or closes, the application does not announce the state change via an aria-live region, nor does it move keyboard focus into the drawer. Users relying on screen readers are not informed that the navigation has become available or unavailable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1596: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` inside open(). Line 1632: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` inside close().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion panel open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ answer changes visible content but does not announce the change via a live region or move focus to the new content. Screen reader users may not realize the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3172: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) || `${item.question} expanded`);` inside open(). Line 3195: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) || `${item.question} collapsed`);` inside close().

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly, quarterly, and annual billing updates the displayed prices visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or which billing period is now active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2930: After updating the plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: contactForm fields missing aria-invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails for contact form fields (full_name, email, subject, message, consent), the inlineValidator marks the field visually as invalid and links the error message via aria-describedby, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3488: In inlineValidator._renderResult, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` in the else branch (invalid case). Line 3482: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` in the if branch (valid case).

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email field missing aria-invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email field validation does not set aria-invalid="true" when the email is invalid, because inlineValidator only manages aria-describedby but not aria-invalid. Assistive technologies cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3488: In inlineValidator._renderResult, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` when result is invalid.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard fields missing aria-invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: Wizard step validation for fields (wizard_name, wizard_email, wizard_plan, wizard_card_name) does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields, preventing screen readers from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3488: In inlineValidator._renderResult, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` when result is invalid.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input in contactForm
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field in the contact form becomes valid, the validation renderer removes the error styling and aria-describedby reference but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3482: In inlineValidator._renderResult, ensure `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` is called when result.valid is true.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialized without an aria-expanded attribute. Since the drawer starts closed, the trigger should have aria-expanded="false" to indicate the initial collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1668: After `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element id. Screen readers cannot establish the relationship between the trigger and the controlled navigation panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1669: After getting _drawer, add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion list, but it lacks an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing the suggestion popup is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2804: After mounting the listbox, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code removes the open CSS class but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree even when visually hidden via CSS, causing screen readers to access off-screen content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1624: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` inside close(). Line 1590: In open(), add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` or `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The user action of toggling the mobile navigation menu changes the visible content significantly (showing/hiding the entire navigation), but the result is not announced via an aria-live region, violating the requirement for status updates on dynamic content changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1596: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation opened');`. Line 1632: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking an FAQ question to expand or collapse the answer is a user action that changes visible content, but the outcome is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users uncertain whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3172: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));`. Line 3195: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters to the product catalogue is a user-initiated action that updates the visible product count and grid, but the updated result count is not announced via an aria-live region, so screen reader users do not know the filter has been applied or how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2398: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Selecting a different billing period (monthly/quarterly/annual) is a user action that updates all price displays on the page, but the change is not announced via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2930: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Billing period changed to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer declares a `_trap` variable but never initializes or activates a focus trap. When the drawer opens, focus is not contained within the navigation, allowing users to tab behind the overlay to the main page content, and focus is not returned to the trigger button when the drawer closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1597: After opening, add `if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`. Line 1633: Before closing, add `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();`.

issuesFound.explanation: The contact form first name input (id="contact-first-name") handles personal data (given-name) but does not have an autocomplete attribute set to "given-name". issuesFound.fix: Line 3451: After getting the field, add `if (_firstNameEl) setAttr(_firstNameEl, 'autocomplete', 'given-name');`.

issuesFound.title: contactLastName input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form last name input (id="contact-last-name") handles personal data (family-name) but lacks the autocomplete="family-name" attribute, preventing proper identification by assistive technologies and browsers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3451: After getting the field, add `if (_lastNameEl) setAttr(_lastNameEl, 'autocomplete', 'family-name');`.

issuesFound.title: contactEmail input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form email input (id="contact-email") handles personal email data but does not specify autocomplete="email", failing to meet WCAG 2.2 success criterion 1.3.5 for input purpose identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3451: After getting the field, add `if (_emailEl) setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`.

issuesFound.title: newsletterEmail input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter subscription email input (id="newsletter-email") collects email addresses but lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, preventing assistive technologies from identifying the input purpose programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3379: After getting _emailEl, add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions omit aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons lack aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period selection buttons (Monthly/Quarterly/Annual) behave as a set of mutually exclusive toggle buttons, but the _syncToggleState function only toggles CSS classes. It does not set aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator fails to set aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the _renderResult function calls srUtils.markInvalid and adds error classes, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute to "true". When validation passes, it does not clear aria-invalid to "false". Screen readers therefore cannot convey the field's validity state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line inlineValidator._renderResult (valid branch): Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` immediately before the call to `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);`.
    Line inlineValidator._renderResult (invalid branch): Insert `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately before the call to `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, the code ensures they have aria-haspopup, but it does not initialise aria-expanded="false" for the closed state. This leaves the initial expanded state ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register: After the line `if (!trigger.hasAttribute('aria-haspopup')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-haspopup', 'true');`, insert `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox", but the required aria-expanded attribute is missing from the initial markup. This prevents screen readers from announcing whether the suggestion list is collapsed or expanded on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init: After the line `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, insert `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Hidden mobileNav drawer not marked with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the code removes CSS classes and scroll-locks the body, but it does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element itself. This can leave the hidden drawer exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` immediately before the call to `_unlockScroll();`.

issuesFound.title: Hidden dropdownMenu panels not marked with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden using the hidden attribute and CSS transitions, but aria-hidden="true" is not applied to the panel element when it is closed, potentially leaving it in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Insert `setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` inside the setTimeout callback or immediately after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: Hidden faqAccordion panels not marked with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is collapsed, the panel receives the hidden attribute after an animation, but aria-hidden="true" is never applied to explicitly remove it from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.close: Insert `setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` inside the animateHeight onComplete callback or immediately after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links marked as current visually indicate the active page but lack aria-current="page", so screen reader users cannot identify which page is current.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element containing skip links lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks in screen reader landmark lists.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation">`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The main navigation landmark lacks an aria-label to distinguish it from other navigation regions (such as footer or mobile navigation).
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label, making it difficult to distinguish from other navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label: `<nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section lacks an aria-labelledby attribute to provide an accessible name for the landmark, even though it contains a heading with id="hero-heading".
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby: `<section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">`

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section lacks an aria-labelledby attribute to name the landmark, despite having a heading with id="stats-heading".
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby: `<section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card uses an article element but lacks aria-labelledby to associate it with the plan name heading (headingId is defined but not used on the article).
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-labelledby: `<article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">`

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing descriptive aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The call-to-action link uses generic text ("Get started", "Start Pro free trial") without an aria-label that includes the plan name, making it indistinguishable from other plan CTAs when read out of context.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label: `<a href={plan.ctaHref} aria-label={`${plan.ctaLabel} for ${plan.name}`} className={['btn', plan.featured ? 'btn--primary' : 'btn--outline', 'btn--md'].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar duplicate list not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for the marquee visual effect is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce the integration list twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden: `<ul role="list" aria-hidden="true" className="integrations-bar__list">`

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★/☆) are not hidden from assistive technology, causing redundant announcements since the container already has an aria-label with the rating value.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden to the inner wrapper span: `<span aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading
issuesFound.explanation: When isLoading is true, the button displays a spinner but does not set aria-busy to communicate the loading state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-busy: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is incorrectly placed on the backdrop overlay instead of the dialog element itself, potentially causing screen readers to treat the backdrop as the modal container.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Remove aria-modal from overlay div and add to dialog div: `<div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} ...>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron indicator (▲/▼) is decorative but not hidden from assistive technology, causing extraneous character announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="nav-link__chevron">`

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is announced as "star" by screen readers because it lacks aria-hidden, even though the "(required)" text is provided via VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden: ` *`
